sections.heading: 1. Why Generics?
sections.content: Generics allow you to write reusable and type-safe code. Using Any requires unsafe casting and can cause ClassCastException at runtime. Generics move these errors to compile-time, making your code safer.

sections.heading: 2. Generic Classes
sections.content: A generic class defines a type parameter using angle brackets <T>. The actual type is specified when creating an instance.

sections.heading: Generic Class Example
sections.content: The Box class can hold any type safely.

sections.heading: Android Usage
sections.content: Generics are used widely in Android, such as ArrayList<String>, LiveData<User>, and Response<T>.

sections.heading: 3. Generic Functions
sections.content: Functions can also define type parameters. The generic type is written before the function name.

sections.heading: Generic Function Example
sections.content: Type is automatically inferred by the compiler.

sections.heading: 4. Generic Constraints (Upper Bounds)
sections.content: Upper bounds restrict what types can be used with generics using a colon (:).

sections.heading: Upper Bound Example
sections.content: This function only accepts Number types.

sections.heading: 5. Variance: out and in
sections.content: Variance defines how generic types behave in inheritance relationships.

sections.heading: Covariance (out)
sections.content: Use out when a class only produces values of type T and does not consume them.

sections.heading: Producer Example
sections.content: Producer returns T but does not accept it.

sections.heading: Contravariance (in)
sections.content: Use in when a class only consumes values of type T.

sections.heading: Consumer Example
sections.content: Consumer accepts T but does not return it.

sections.heading: 6. Real-World Android Example: Generic Base Adapter
sections.content: A reusable RecyclerView adapter can use generics to support multiple data types.

sections.heading: BaseAdapter Example
sections.content: T can represent any model type such as User, Product, or Notification.

sections.heading: Summary
items: Generic classes and functions improve reusability.
Upper bounds (<T : Type>) restrict allowed types.
